title: Best way to track client appointments and payments for multiple coaches and clients?

posts.author: alexar (Alexa Reardon)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have multiple coaches, which each have multiple clients.

All clients are in one board (linked to some other boards), but the main board we need help with is the one wherein we track their appointments and payments.

I’m looking for advice on the best way to do this, whether it be with marketplace apps or just out-of-the-box Monday automations.

What we need to track and other requirements:

  • Each appointment a client has, added by coaches as they go
  • Whether they’ve prepaid at all
  • Whether they have outstanding invoices
  • Running count of all appointments the client has ever had
  • Whether they’ve paid, and for which appointments
  • Limit visibility of payment info to coaches
  • Ability to add invoice files as needed, restrict visibility to coaches
  • Ability to sort by Coach (currently doing this with a dropdown and using Group By)

One concern is that it needs to be simple enough for coaches to navigate. We also don’t want appointments to build up too much and create lag.

How we’re currently doing it is terrible, and I’ve been designing a new workflow that involves using subitems as the appointments, which are then automatically tracked in the parent item. They are recorded once paid to the parent item (client item) updates so we can archive the subitem (appointment) eventually/as needed. This doesn’t feel like a perfect solution though so I wonder if there’s a better way to do this.

We don’t want to have too many boards talking to one another either as it confuses the users. The previous way we were doing it involved having columns for each month.. you can imagine how clogged up that got and how hard it was to track!

Thanks for any input!

posts.author: Kevincontrera13 (kevin - Flusss.net)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alexa! Based on what you’ve shared, I’d recommend using monday.com CRM to structure your workflow more effectively. Here’s a simplified setup:

  • Use one main Clients board to store all client details.
  • Create individual boards per coach where each item represents a client appointment.
  • Link each coach board to the Clients board (using connected boards), allowing relevant info to sync.
  • Track activities and payments using the Email & Activities section within the CRM — great for logging communication and notes. here you can see how it works:
    https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WBts0aGg4Fw
  • Use automations so that once an appointment is marked as complete or paid, the data is copied to a separate billing board — this way coaches don’t see sensitive payment info or invoices.
  • You can upload invoice files there, and control visibility with item permissions.
  • Sorting/filtering by coach is simple using a general dashboard or filtered views, and keeping coach boards separate helps reduce lag and confusion.

I think this setup covers all your listed needs and keeps things coach-friendly. Let me know if you’d like help building out the automations or setting this up! I would love to help
